original_hypothesis.title: AgentToken.addLiquidityPool can whitelist arbitrary contract as LP, enabling tax-on-transfer manipulation and forced taxation on normal transfers
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: AgentToken considers transfers taxable when either side is a recognized liquidity pool. onlyOwnerOrFactory can add any contract address (code.length>0) to _liquidityPools. A privileged actor could (intentionally or accidentally) mark common addresses (routers, vaults, custodians) as LPs, causing buys/sells logic to misfire and apply taxes in unintended contexts, potentially locking users or causing excessive tax accrual.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: While privileged, this is a practical operational footgun that can break transfer semantics and harm users. It is also relevant to validate that no other code path can add pools without proper authorization.

proof_of_signal.pattern: Tax application uses isLiquidityPool(from)||isLiquidityPool(to); admin can add arbitrary contract to liquidity pool set

preconditions: Owner or factory (privileged) adds an address to liquidity pools
Users interact with that address (transfers, custody movements, staking integrations)

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: admin-controlled classification impacts core transfer logic
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: Misclassification can turn ordinary transfers into taxed buys/sells, changing balances and pending swaps unexpectedly
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: No validation that pool is actual AMM pair for (this,pairToken); no factory verification

analysis_questions: Is addLiquidityPool used in production to support multiple DEXs, and how is it governed?
Could pool misclassification cause tax to exceed expected bounds and lead to user loss?
Are there integrations (staking, vaults) whose addresses could be mistakenly added, causing systemic issues?
validation_requirements: Confirm no unprivileged path can call addLiquidityPool
Test transfer behavior when a non-pair address is added as liquidity pool
Assess whether tax accounting (projectTaxPendingSwap) remains consistent under misclassification

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Privileged operator mistakenly or maliciously adds a major contract (e.g., staking vault) as LP. All transfers to/from it are treated as buys/sells and taxed, siphoning balances to the token contract and possibly triggering autoswaps, harming user balances and protocol operations.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Moderate user losses due to unexpected taxation; potential protocol disruption
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Possible by removing liquidity pool entry, but losses/tax side effects persist

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: Missing tests ensuring only actual pairs are added as pools

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: Unexpected tax events on transfers not involving actual AMMs

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
